title: simplistic property paths result in errors despite direct edges...
description:
A query like
ASK { dbr:Braveheart (<>|!<>)+ dbr:Mel_Gibson }

results in:
Virtuoso 42000 Error TN...: Exceeded 1000000000 bytes in transitive temp memory.  use t_distinct, t_max or more T_MAX_memory options to limit the search or increase the pool

try yourself
Thing is: there are several direct edges, so i guess this is a bug in the query planner...
SELECT * { dbr:Braveheart ?p dbr:Mel_Gibson }

see also: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/43269978/use-sparql-property-path-on-dbpedia
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE actors (id Int32, name String, birth_year Int32, is_active UInt8) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
CREATE TABLE movies (id Int32, title String, release_year Int32, director_id Int32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO actors (id, name, birth_year, is_active) VALUES (1, 'Mel Gibson', 1956, 1);
INSERT INTO movies (id, title, release_year, director_id) VALUES (1, 'Braveheart', 1995, 1);
SELECT * FROM movies WHERE director_id IN (SELECT id FROM actors WHERE is_active = 1);
key_bug_features: Property paths with direct edges cause memory errors
